stem: A sequence of proposed vertex degrees is \( ([[d1]], [[d2]], [[d3]], [[d4]], [[d5]], [[d6]]) \). By the handshake lemma, a degree sequence can only be realized by an actual graph if its sum is even. What is the sum of this degree sequence?
solution: The sum of the degree sequence is \( [[d1]]+[[d2]]+[[d3]]+[[d4]]+[[d5]]+[[d6]] = [[answer]] \) (the sequence is graphical-by-parity only if this is even, since it must equal twice the edge count).
distractor_rationales: This leaves out one of the six degrees from the sum.
This divides the correct sum by 2, confusing the degree sum with the edge count.
This multiplies three of the degrees instead of summing all six.
This overshoots the correct sum by 1.
This undershoots the correct sum by 1.
This overshoots the correct sum by 2.

stem: A sequence of proposed vertex degrees is \( ([[d1]], [[d2]], [[d3]], [[d4]], [[d5]], [[d6]]) \). In any graph, the number of vertices with odd degree must be even. How many of these six proposed degrees are odd?
solution: Checking each degree's parity and counting the odd ones among \( [[d1]], [[d2]], [[d3]], [[d4]], [[d5]], [[d6]] \) gives [[answer]] (this count must be even for the sequence to be graphical).
distractor_rationales: This only checks the first three degrees instead of all six.
This counts the EVEN-degree vertices instead of the odd-degree ones.
This adds two of the raw degree values instead of counting how many are odd.
This overshoots the correct odd-degree count by 1.
This overshoots the correct odd-degree count by 2.
This undershoots the correct odd-degree count by 1.

stem: Five vertices of a graph have degrees \( [[d1]], [[d2]], [[d3]], [[d4]], [[d5]] \), and a sixth vertex has some unknown degree \( x \ge 0 \). Since the total degree sum of any graph must be even, what is the SMALLEST nonnegative integer value \( x \) can take?
solution: The known five degrees sum to \( [[d1]]+[[d2]]+[[d3]]+[[d4]]+[[d5]] \); for the total (including \(x\)) to be even, \(x\) must have the same parity as this sum, and the smallest nonnegative such \(x\) is \([[answer]]\) (either \(0\) if the known sum is already even, or \(1\) if it is odd).
distractor_rationales: This reports the raw sum of the five known degrees instead of the needed parity of \(x\).
This adds one to the correct minimal value.
This adds two to the correct minimal value.
This flips the required parity, picking the opposite of the correct minimal value.
This guesses a fixed value unrelated to the actual parity of the known sum.
This guesses another fixed value unrelated to the actual parity of the known sum.
